title: Sensor Data Quality Trending
spl:
index=ot sourcetype IN ("edge_hub:metrics","modbus:readings","opcua:telemetry") earliest=-30d@d
| eval bad=if(match(lower(coalesce(quality,data_quality)),"(?i)bad|invalid|stale") OR isnull(metric_value),1,0)
| timechart span=1d sum(bad) as bad_reads count as total_reads
| eval bad_pct=round(100*bad_reads/nullif(total_reads,0),2)
| trendline sma7(bad_pct) as quality_trend
| eventstats avg(bad_pct) as run_avg
| eval delta=round(bad_pct - run_avg,2)
description: Missing or stale readings break historians, dashboards, and ML features; daily trending exposes ingestion gaps early so teams can fix collectors, radios, or brokers before safety or quality thresholds are blind.
value: Missing or stale readings break historians, dashboards, and ML features; daily trending exposes ingestion gaps early so teams can fix collectors, radios, or brokers before safety or quality thresholds are blind.
implementation: Map your quality field (`good`, `192` OPC status, Modbus exception flags). If quality is not present, infer staleness with `streamstats` per `device_id` and `tag` using gaps between `_time` greater than `3×` the expected poll interval from a lookup. Dedupe by `device_id`+`tag` to avoid double counting. Tune thresholds per line speed—fast PLCs need tighter windows than environmental sensors.
visualization: Line chart (daily bad or stale % with trend), Stacked bar (bad readings by site), Table (worst devices by gap count).
knownFalsePositives: Quality shifts when calibrations run, sensors are replaced, or quality limits are re-tightened for campaigns.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Edge Hub, Modbus/OPC-UA TAs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype IN ("edge_hub:metrics","edge_hub:events","modbus:readings","opcua:telemetry")`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map your quality field (`good`, `192` OPC status, Modbus exception flags). If quality is not present, infer staleness with `streamstats` per `device_id` and `tag` using gaps between `_time` greater than `3×` the expected poll interval from a lookup. Dedupe by `device_id`+`tag` to avoid double counting. Tune thresholds per line speed—fast PLCs need tighter windows than environmental sensors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype IN ("edge_hub:metrics","modbus:readings","opcua:telemetry") earliest=-30d@d
| eval bad=if(match(lower(coalesce(quality,data_quality)),"(?i)bad|invalid|stale") OR isnull(metric_value),1,0)
| timechart span=1d sum(bad) as bad_reads count as total_reads
| eval bad_pct=round(100*bad_reads/nullif(total_reads,0),2)
| trendline sma7(bad_pct) as quality_trend
| eventstats avg(bad_pct) as run_avg
| eval delta=round(bad_pct - run_avg,2)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Sensor Data Quality Trending** — Missing or stale readings break historians, dashboards, and ML features; daily trending exposes ingestion gaps early so teams can fix collectors, radios, or brokers before safety or quality thresholds are blind.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype IN ("edge_hub:metrics","edge_hub:events","modbus:readings","opcua:telemetry")`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Edge Hub, Modbus/OPC-UA TAs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bad** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bad_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Sensor Data Quality Trending**): trendline sma7(bad_pct) as quality_trend
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over at least 30 days when possible, then compare trends with operations views, edge metrics, or source historians. Tie step changes in the trend to known campaigns, seasons, or upgrades.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (daily bad or stale % with trend), Stacked bar (bad readings by site), Table (worst devices by gap count).
grandmaExplanation: We trend data quality and gap rates so quality drift is visible in the run-up to scrap or unplanned stops.
